metrics.description: The candidate artifact passes every applicable oracle check. This is the benchmark pass/fail metric and is only applicable when a candidate root is evaluated.
The candidate satisfies the active adapter's artifact contract, including metadata, APIs, schemas, docs, and runtime binding references.
The candidate can execute or simulate adapter-defined runtime actions and produce expected state changes.
The candidate UI is wired for interaction through the adapter runtime bridge and passes available UI/script checks.
The candidate export or migration metadata is structurally valid and ready for adapter roundtrip checks.
The candidate does not include forbidden paths, secret-looking content, escaping symlinks, or unrelated unsafe artifacts.
The case definition, fixtures, and optional builder baseline are valid. This is the self-check metric for benchmark authors.
The case definition has the required schema, id, capabilities, input, instruction, and oracle fields.
All contract and initial-project fixtures referenced by the case exist.
For contract-backed create cases, the active adapter builder produces output that satisfies the case oracle.
The builder baseline exposes all required API/tool ids.
The builder baseline HTML contains required Workbench bridge bindings such as window.oob.callApi and onProjectUpdated.
Agent-backed baseline APIs declare non-empty capabilities.
The candidate artifact includes required files such as project.json, frontend/html/index.html, and Project docs.
The candidate project.json exposes all required API/tool ids.
The candidate HTML contains required runtime bridge calls and stable data-oob identifiers.
The candidate JSON metadata matches required JSON pointer assertions, such as adapter-specific export metadata.
The candidate output does not create forbidden paths such as .github, AGENTS.md, keystores, or signing files.
The candidate output does not contain forbidden retired skill ids or sensitive credential names.

reporting.aggregate: mean pass rate over applicable cases
